title: 'Staggering' I was not told Mandelson failed vetting, says PM
summary_cn: 英国首相斯塔默表示，他作为首相竟未被告知曼德尔森勋爵未通过初步安全审查，这令人震惊。反对党要求斯塔默辞职，因其在任命曼德尔森为美国大使一事上被指误导议会。政府称当时无部长知晓审查结果，斯塔默计划周一在议会说明全部事实。
paragraphs_en: Sir Keir Starmer has said it's "staggering" that he, as prime minister, was not told about Lord Peter Mandelson failing to pass initial security vetting checks.

The prime minister is facing calls to resign over the revelation that vetting officers recommended against Lord Mandelson's being appointed as US ambassador, but that recommendation was overruled by the Foreign Office.
Sir Olly Robbins, who was two weeks into the job heading up the Foreign Office at the time, was effectively sacked by Sir Keir last night.
Opposition parties are calling for Sir Keir to resign, with Tory leader Kemi Badenoch saying the explanation so far is "completely preposterous" and "all roads lead to resignation".

At a press conference in Westminster, she said Sir Keir "cannot hope" to continue leading the country due to his "incompetence," accusing him of being "so blinded by his own righteousness that he cannot see what everybody else can see".
The Tory leader said she was "considering every parliamentary option" for removing the prime minister but called on Labour MPs to "do the right thing" and get rid of him.
"I do not have enough Conservatives MPs to win a vote of no confidence," she said.
"The people who can do that, who can make this happen are Labour MPs."
Lord Mandelson was announced as the UK's ambassador to the US in December 2024, before in-depth vetting had been carried out.
He formally took up the role in February 2025, and was sacked seven months later over his ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
On Thursday, a Guardian investigation revealed Mandelson had been appointed despite failing initial vetting.
Later on Thursday evening, the government said no minister had been informed of that decision at the time, and the PM had only discovered it this week.
Speaking to journalists in Paris on Friday, Sir Keir said: "That I wasn't told that Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting when he was appointed is staggering.
"That I wasn't told that he had failed security vetting when I was telling Parliament that due process had been followed is unforgivable.
"Not only was I not told, no minister was told, and I'm absolutely furious about that.
"What I intend to do is to go to Parliament on Monday to set out all the relevant facts in true transparency, so Parliament has the full picture."
He added: "Number 10 was not told that he had failed security vetting - that is completely unacceptable."
Senior minister Darren Jones earlier said Sir Keir had not misled MPs when he said due process had been followed, and therefore would not be resigning.

Jones told BBC Radio 4's Today programme, there was no obligation in the rules for ministers to be told about security vetting decisions when Lord Mandelson was appointed, something he said he had now changed.
BBC News understands the United Kingdom Security Vetting service gave the Foreign Office an explicit recommendation not to approve Lord Mandelson's vetting.
It's understood that UKSV, which carried out the work on behalf of the Cabinet Office, presented the Foreign Office with a list of potential risks as well as a recommendation summing up those risks.
That recommendation can fall into one of three categories, described by sources as "yes", "yes with caveats" and "no".
Sources say the recommendation given to the Foreign Office by the vetting services was a "no".
The Foreign Office is believed to be the only government department in Whitehall with the authority to overrule such a recommendation.
Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey said the PM's explanation "just doesn't stack up," adding: "I am afraid he is going to have to go."

Sir Ed has called for an investigation by the Privileges Committee, the same process used to hold Boris Johnson to account over the Partygate scandal, into whether Sir Keir intentionally misled Parliament.
Lib Dem Cabinet Office spokesperson Lisa Smart has written to Sir Keir's ethics adviser Sir Laurie Magnus to urge him to investigate.

Foreign Affairs select committee chair Dame Emily Thornberry has invited Sir Olly to give evidence on Tuesday next week, the second time he will have been quizzed about his involvement in the Mandelson scandal.
Labour MP Dame Emily told Sky News: "Perhaps he can tell us… was it his own idea, or was he being leant on elsewhere?
"Or was he, being a civil servant, was he getting direction from elsewhere, and if so, by whom?"

Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar, on the campaign trail in Edinburgh, told journalists he stood by his call for Sir Keir to resign over the Mandelson scandal, saying there were questions for Downing Street and the UK Government to answer.

He said: "I stated my position, I stand by it, I don't recoil for it, the Mandelson scandal was the tipping point for me," adding he believed Lord Mandelson to be "a traitor to his party and country".
The SNP, Green Party and Reform UK have also called for Sir Keir to resign.

paragraphs_cn: 基尔·斯塔默爵士表示，作为首相，他竟未被告知彼得·曼德尔森勋爵未能通过初步安全审查，这令人“震惊”。
由于有消息披露，审查官员曾建议不任命曼德尔森勋爵为美国大使，但这一建议被外交部推翻，首相因此面临辞职呼声。
奥利·罗宾斯爵士当时刚上任外交部负责人两周，昨晚被基尔爵士实际解职。
反对党呼吁基尔爵士辞职，保守党领袖凯米·巴德诺克称迄今的解释“完全荒谬”，并说“所有道路都通向辞职”。
在威斯敏斯特的新闻发布会上，她表示基尔爵士因“无能”而“无法指望”继续领导国家，指责他“被自己的正义感蒙蔽，看不到别人都能看到的东西”。
保守党领袖称她正在“考虑所有议会选项”以罢免首相，但呼吁工党议员“做正确的事”并摆脱他。
她说：“我没有足够的保守党议员来赢得不信任投票。”
“能做到这一点、让此事发生的人是工党议员。”
曼德尔森勋爵于2024年12月被宣布为英国驻美国大使，当时尚未进行深入审查。
他于2025年2月正式上任，七个月后因与已故性犯罪者杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的联系被解职。
周四，《卫报》的一项调查披露，曼德尔森尽管未能通过初步审查仍被任命。
周四晚些时候，政府表示当时没有部长被告知这一决定，首相本周才得知此事。
周五在巴黎对记者讲话时，基尔爵士说：“我未被告知彼得·曼德尔森在任命时未能通过安全审查，这令人震惊。”
“我未被告知他未能通过安全审查，而我当时告诉议会已遵循正当程序，这是不可原谅的。”
“不仅我未被告知，没有部长被告知，我对此感到非常愤怒。”
“我打算周一去议会，以真正的透明度陈述所有相关事实，让议会了解全貌。”
他补充道：“唐宁街10号未被告知他未能通过安全审查——这完全不可接受。”
高级部长达伦·琼斯早些时候表示，基尔爵士说已遵循正当程序时并未误导议员，因此不会辞职。
琼斯告诉BBC广播4台的《今日》节目，规则中没有义务在曼德尔森勋爵任命时告知部长安全审查决定，他说现已改变此规定。
BBC新闻了解到，英国安全审查服务部门曾向外交部明确建议不批准曼德尔森勋爵的审查。
据了解，代表内阁办公室进行审查的英国安全审查服务部门向外交部提供了一份潜在风险清单和总结这些风险的建议。
该建议可分为三类，消息人士描述为“是”、“有条件的是”和“否”。
消息人士称，审查服务部门给外交部的建议是“否”。
据信，外交部是白厅唯一有权推翻此类建议的政府部门。
自由民主党领袖埃德·戴维爵士表示，首相的解释“站不住脚”，并补充道：“恐怕他必须下台。”
埃德爵士呼吁特权委员会进行调查，该程序曾用于追究鲍里斯·约翰逊在“派对门”丑闻中的责任，以查明基尔爵士是否故意误导议会。
自由民主党内阁办公室发言人丽莎·斯马特已致信基尔爵士的道德顾问劳里·马格努斯爵士，敦促他进行调查。
外交事务特别委员会主席艾米丽·索恩伯里夫人已邀请奥利爵士下周周二作证，这将是他第二次就曼德尔森丑闻中的参与接受质询。
工党议员艾米丽夫人告诉天空新闻：“也许他能告诉我们……这是他的主意，还是他受到其他地方的压力？”
“或者，作为公务员，他是否从其他地方得到指示，如果是，是谁？”
苏格兰工党领袖阿纳斯·萨瓦尔在爱丁堡竞选活动中告诉记者，他坚持呼吁基尔爵士因曼德尔森丑闻辞职，称唐宁街和英国政府需要回答问题。
他说：“我表明了我的立场，我坚持它，我不退缩，曼德尔森丑闻是我的转折点，”并补充他认为曼德尔森勋爵是“其政党和国家的叛徒”。
苏格兰民族党、绿党和改革英国党也呼吁基尔爵士辞职。

keywords.context_sentence: That I wasn't told that Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting when he was appointed is staggering.
keywords.context_translation: 我被告知彼得·曼德尔森在任命时未能通过安全审查，这令人震惊。

keywords.definition_en: The process of examining someone's background or qualifications.
keywords.context_sentence: The prime minister is facing calls to resign over the revelation that vetting officers recommended against Lord Mandelson's being appointed as US ambassador.
keywords.context_translation: 首相因被揭露审查官员建议反对曼德尔森勋爵被任命为美国大使而面临辞职呼声。

keywords.definition_en: To reject or disallow a decision or proposal.
keywords.context_sentence: The prime minister is facing calls to resign over the revelation that vetting officers recommended against Lord Mandelson's being appointed as US ambassador, but that recommendation was overruled by the Foreign Office.
keywords.context_translation: 首相因被揭露审查官员建议反对曼德尔森勋爵被任命为美国大使而面临辞职呼声，但该建议被外交部驳回。

keywords.context_sentence: Opposition parties are calling for Sir Keir to resign, with Tory leader Kemi Badenoch saying the explanation so far is 'completely preposterous' and 'all roads lead to resignation'.
keywords.context_translation: 反对党呼吁基尔爵士辞职，保守党领袖凯米·巴德诺克表示目前的解释“完全荒谬”，且“所有道路都指向辞职”。

keywords.definition_en: Lack of ability or skill to do something successfully.
keywords.context_sentence: At a press conference in Westminster, she said Sir Keir 'cannot hope' to continue leading the country due to his 'incompetence'.
keywords.context_translation: 在威斯敏斯特的新闻发布会上，她表示基尔爵士因其“无能”而无法继续领导国家。

keywords.definition_en: The quality of being open and honest; easy to perceive or understand.
keywords.context_sentence: 'What I intend to do is to go to Parliament on Monday to set out all the relevant facts in true transparency, so Parliament has the full picture.'
keywords.context_translation: “我打算周一去议会，以真正的透明度阐述所有相关事实，以便议会了解全貌。”

keywords.definition_en: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion.
keywords.context_sentence: BBC News understands the United Kingdom Security Vetting service gave the Foreign Office an explicit recommendation not to approve Lord Mandelson's vetting.
keywords.context_translation: BBC新闻了解到，英国安全审查服务向外交部提出了明确的建议，不批准曼德尔森勋爵的审查。

keywords.definition_en: The power or right to give orders, make decisions, or enforce obedience.
keywords.context_sentence: The Foreign Office is believed to be the only government department in Whitehall with the authority to overrule such a recommendation.
keywords.context_translation: 据信，外交部是白厅唯一有权否决此类建议的政府部门。
